title: Clone engineer
content:

{{Quote|Never say never, Ahsoka—especially where clone engineers are concerned.|[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], to [[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]]|<ref name="Repel All Boarders!">{{TCWComicCite|6.18|Repel All Boarders!}}</ref>}}
'''Clone engineers''', also known as '''combat engineer clone troopers''', were [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] that specialized in military [[Engineer/Legends|engineering]], repairs, and demolitions work, filling a supportive role in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] military during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] against the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Following the [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] engineers continued to serve under the Imperial regime.

==Description==
===Overview===
[[File:CloneEngineers.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Three clone engineers of the Combat Engineer Battalion.]]
[[Cloning/Legends|Clone]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]],<ref name="SW:BF2" /> also called combat engineer [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]],<ref name="Hasbro combat engineer">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Saga Collection|pack=#068: Combat Engineer Clone Trooper|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TSC068engineer.asp}}</ref> were clone [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] that specialized in military engineering—performing field repairs on vehicles, structures,<ref name="SW:BF2" /> [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]], [[Droid/Legends|droids]], and general equipment for the Grand Army.<ref name="SW:BF2 Hasbro">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: 30th Anniversary Collection|pack=''Star Wars: Battlefront II'' Clone Pack|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/tacbf2clones.asp}}</ref> 

Selected randomly on [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] to become engineers, clone combat engineers became attuned to vehicles and their idiosyncrasies during their training.<ref name="SW:BF2 Prima">[[Star Wars Battlefront II: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Battlefront II'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> They were skilled at construction and equipment modifications, with one group of engineers being able to reconfigure an [[A5-RX battle tank]] into a [[A5-RX battle tank|A5-RX spread-missile battle platform]] in the midst of battle.<ref name="Repel All Boarders!">{{TCWComicCite|6.18|Repel All Boarders!}}</ref> Clone combat engineers filled a supportive role in the Grand Army, often being assigned to armored [[Division/Legends|divisions]] and units that used heavy vehicles or [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] emplacements.<ref name="Hasbro combat engineer" /> Clone engineers were also skilled at demolitions,<ref name="SW:TCW video game" /> and were used to [[Sensor/Legends|scan]] for and dispose of roadside explosives during travel.<ref name="Dark Times: ASR5" />

Different units fielded clone engineers during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]—including the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]],<ref name="SW:BF2" /> the [[38th Armored Division]],<ref name="Hasbro combat engineer" /> and the 38th Division's [[Combat Engineer Battalion]]—which was made up entirely of clone combat engineers.<ref name="TotG" />

===Equipment and armor===
[[File:Faie-Saleucami.png|thumb|right|300px|Faie's customized clone engineer armor.]]
Clone engineers wore both standard [[Phase II clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase II clone trooper armor]]<ref name="Hasbro combat engineer" /> and a modified version of the [[Armor/Legends|armor]], with a [[ribcage]]-shaped chestplate and a more rounded [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]] design—featuring two visors instead of a single T-shaped one.<ref name="SW:BF2" /> The armor could be customized to denote unit affiliation, as was the case with the 38th Armored Division's [[Color/Legends|brown]] markings.<ref name="Hasbro combat engineer" /> [[Clone Commander/Legends|Clone trooper commander]] [[Faie/Legends|Faie]] customized [[Gender/Legends|his]] clone engineer armor further, giving it a white cloak.<ref name="Republic 74">[[Republic 74|''Republic'' 74]]</ref>

Combat engineer clone troopers wielded different [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] during the war—including the [[DC-15S/Legends|DC-15S]] [[Blaster carbine/Legends|blaster carbine]],<ref name="Hasbro combat engineer" /> the [[DC-15A blaster rifle/Legends|DC-15A blaster rifle]],<ref name="Hasbro DVD">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (toy line)|pack=Clone Troopers (Commemorative Episode III DVD Collection)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/rots05dvdsetColl3.asp}}</ref> [[Shotgun/Legends|shotguns]], and [[detonation pack]]s.<ref name="SW:BF2" /> Clone engineers also made use of supportive equipment, such as the [[F-187 fusioncutter]] which they used for repairs, and [[ammo canister|ammo]] and [[bacta canister]]s which were given out by the engineers to other troopers during battle.<ref name="SW:BF2" /> Clone engineers units could be deployed from [[Republic Troop Transport/Legends|Republic Troop Transports]].<ref name="SW:TCW video game" />

==History==
===Clone Wars===
[[File:Hasbrocloneengineer.png|thumb|left|170px|A clone engineer.]]
Active as early as [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> clone engineers participated in the [[Battle of Thule]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], where they were deployed to destroy multiple [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], leaving them vulnerable to attack.<ref name="SW:TCW video game" /> One year later, in [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]],<ref name="RaB Timeline">[[Repel All Boarders!]] is set around the time of "[[Heroes on Both Sides]]" ([[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]]), which marks the debut of revamped appearances for several main characters. Considering that the [[Star Wars comic books|comics]] featured in [[Star Wars Comic|''Star Wars Comic UK'']] are published in chronological order, and that "[[Burn the Behemoth!]]" is the first comic in the series to feature the new character models, it can be concluded that "Repel All Boarders!" takes place in 21 BBY.</ref> the [[38th Armored Division]]'s clone engineers, including [[Clone Sergeant/Legends|clone trooper sergeant]] [[Gaffa]], helped [[Skirmish aboard the Twilight|repel]] Separatist boarders on the [[Twilight/Legends|''Twilight'']] with a modified A5-RX spread-missile battle platform of their own creation.<ref name="Repel All Boarders!" /> 

Clone combat engineers continued to see service throughout the war, participating in the [[Battle of Kashyyyk/Legends|Battle of Kashyyyk]], the [[Battle of Mygeeto]], and the [[Battle of Utapau/Legends|Battle of Utapau]].<ref name="SW:BF2" />

===Rise of the Galactic Empire===
[[File:Imperialcloneengineer.png|thumb|right|200px|An Imperial clone engineer reports to Darth Vader.]]
Following the activation of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] by [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="SWE3">{{Film|III}}</ref> clone engineers of the 501st Legion participated in [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|Operation: Knightfall]], helping execute [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="SW:BF2" /> 

After the [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in 19 BBY,<ref name="SWE3" /> clone engineers continued to serve as Imperial [[clone stormtrooper]]s under the new regime, finding explosives and disposing of them for [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Vader]], prior to an [[Ambush at Kestavel|ambush]] on [[Kestavel]] where they were all [[Death/Legends|killed]] after being [[Fire/Legends|ignited]] with [[Engine/Legends|engine]] [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]].<ref name="Dark Times: ASR5" />

==Behind the scenes==
Clone engineers first appeared in the [[2002]] [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (video game)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' video game]], developed by [[Pandemic Studios]] and published by [[LucasArts]].<ref name="SW:TCW video game" />

Clone engineers were originally designed as different pieces of concept art of [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] Faie, created for the [[2005]] film {{Film|III}}.<ref name="SWE3 art">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith]]''</ref> Although the designs were never used in the film, they were later adapted into the look of clone engineers for the 2005 [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'', also created by Pandemic Studios and published by LucasArts.<ref name="SW:BF2" />

==Appearances==
